talk_title: Highlights of Recent Clinical Trials in Precision Oncology and Emerging Therapies

speaker.name: Dr. Kathy Dombrowsk
speaker.affiliation: University of California, San Francisco, Department of Oncology

targets.dependency_context: KRAS G12C mutant NSCLC after prior KRAS G12C inhibitor
FBXW7‑mutant ovarian cancer with cyclin E1 amplification
Combination with T‑DXd in HER2‑positive tumors
Folate receptor‑expressing endometrial and ovarian cancers

biomarkers.name: ctDNA clearance
biomarkers.finding: 73% of patients treated with zoldonrasib cleared ctDNA

biomarkers.name: Cyclin E1 amplification
biomarkers.finding: Associated with 60% ORR to VE‑1 + PKMYT combination in ovarian cancer

clinical_data.population: KRAS G12C mutant NSCLC after prior G12C inhibitor
FBXW7‑mutant ovarian cancer with cyclin E1 amplification
High‑risk smoldering multiple myeloma treated with cilta‑cel

MOA_summary: KRAS G12D covalent inhibitor zoldonrasib forms a tricomplex that blocks RAS‑RAF signaling, achieving high ORR and ctDNA clearance in NSCLC. Pan‑RAS PROTAC deraxone rasib degrades mutant and wild‑type RAS, synergizing with gemcitabine/nab‑paclitaxel in pancreatic cancer. VE‑1 and PKMYT inhibitors arrest G2/M by targeting CDK1, showing activity in FBXW7‑mutant ovarian cancer with cyclin E1 amplification. ADCs (T‑DXd, CLDN6‑ADC, ZW91) deliver topoisomerase or DNA‑damaging payloads, enhanced by sequential PARP inhibition. CAR‑T (cilta‑cel) provides durable MRD‑negative remissions in smoldering myeloma. Emerging targets (CCR8, mesothelin) showed limited efficacy, highlighting resistance mechanisms.
key_findings: Zoldonrasib achieved a 52% ORR and median PFS of 11 months in KRAS G12D mutant NSCLC (n=40) with only minimal grade 3 toxicities.
Deraxone rasib combined with gemcitabine/nab‑paclitaxel yielded a 59% ORR and 90% disease control in first‑line metastatic KRAS‑mutant pancreatic cancer, albeit with 73% grade 3+ adverse events.
Elacestrant produced a 32% partial response rate in KRAS G12C NSCLC patients who had progressed on prior G12C inhibitors.
VE‑1 plus PKMYT inhibition resulted in a 60% ORR in FBXW7‑mutant ovarian cancer harboring cyclin E1 amplification, demonstrating target‑driven efficacy.
Sequential combination of trastuzumab deruxtecan with olaparib achieved a 54% overall response rate, supporting schedule‑optimized ADC‑PARP synergy.
Claudin‑6 and folate receptor ADCs each generated >50% response rates across multiple tumor types, even in low‑expressing cohorts.
Cilta‑cel induced 100% MRD negativity and objective responses in high‑risk smoldering myeloma with no grade 3+ CRS, indicating curative potential in pre‑malignancy.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Mesothelin CAR‑T trials showed low response rates due to tumor microenvironment immunosuppression and T‑cell exhaustion.
CCR8‑targeting antibodies yielded single‑digit response rates, suggesting inadequate target engagement or compensatory pathways.

combination_strategies.rationale: Chemotherapy backbone may enhance tumor dependence on RAS signaling, improving PROTAC efficacy
Topoisomerase‑based ADC creates DNA lesions that are synthetically lethal with PARP inhibition
Dual blockade of EGFR and KRAS G12C may overcome resistance mechanisms
combination_strategies.phenotype: Partial responses observed in KRAS G12C colorectal and pancreatic cancers

open_questions: What molecular features define the subset of KRAS G12D NSCLC patients who achieve durable ctDNA clearance?
How can the high grade 3+ toxicity of pan‑RAS PROTAC combos be mitigated while preserving efficacy?
What biomarkers predict response to VE‑1/PKMYT therapy beyond cyclin E1 amplification?
Can ADC payload diversification overcome the class‑wide resistance observed with topoisomerase‑based ADCs?
